other_observers: Dan Walcher, Byron Brown; Anne Esson, reported & sent poor photos to Hugh Kingery.
description: Small, Siskin-sized bird, finch tail, many with very rosy or pink markings on breast & body, distinct red yamake on males. Females much more muted coloring. Will send photos from darkish day if they can be attached or will email separately. Distinctive little goatee like dark coloring on chin of males & around bills. Females predominantly streaky brown backs, both with light-colored breasts where not red or pink depending on gender. This rosy coloring grew more pronounced over 6 wks. when I observed these birds, presumably the same flock.Behaviors: I have a sunflower seed feeder in winter & a thistle sock I put out after Christmas. The first pair or two of Redpolls showed up December 29th or so. Soon there were as many as 20 which arrived always just as a snow storm was about to hit or had come in overnight in East Vail (8500' elevation at my roofline, feeder off 2nd floor deck outside window by kitchen sink). They appeared several times a week till I moved out Feb. 15th including that day.The Redpolls were hungry, greedily so, monopolizing feeders, but I did not see them actively being aggressive to chickadees, House Finches, or others. They just seemed to crowd out the others from feeder and deck railings.Call: Mostly eating, fussing at other birds.Plumage: Moving into breeding
